row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) maintains its top spot with a revenue of 716.9B USD, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by 3.7B USD. The gap between the leader and third-ranked UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH) is significant at 269.3B USD.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a staggering net income of $132.2B USD, outpacing NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) by $12.1B USD. This significant gap highlights GOOGL's substantial dominance in the industry.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with Total Assets of 4.4T USD, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at 3.4T USD, indicating a substantial difference in scale between the two financial giants.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) holds the top spot with $415.3B in Total Equity, a significant lead over Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) with $411.1B, indicating a narrow margin between the two leaders.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with Total Liabilities of $4.1T USD, a significant gap from the second-place Bank of America Corporation (BAC) with $3.1T USD in liabilities. This $1T USD gap highlights the substantial difference in financial leverage between the two banking giants.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap of $103.9B over Bank of America Corporation (BAC) in second place. This substantial lead underscores JPM's strong liquidity position.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of $360.5B, a significant gap of over $120B from the second-place Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) with $240.3B in Gross Profit.
row1.operating_income_ebit: Apple Inc. (AAPL) leads the pack with an Operating Income Ebit of 133.1B USD, a significant 61% gap from the second-place NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) at 130.4B USD. This substantial gap underscores AAPL's dominance in the sector.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate revenue generation, accounting for 43.1% of total revenue with 976.4B USD and 937.6B USD respectively. This significant concentration in two sectors highlights the industry's reliance on these sectors for growth.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the sector distribution by Total Assets, accounting for 64.1% of the total. This concentration is nearly 2.5 times larger than the next closest sector, Technology and Communication Services.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape with a 39.2% share, nearly doubling the next closest sector, Communication Services. This concentration highlights the significant profitability of tech giants like Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet (GOOGL).

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin outpaces UNH's 2.7%, highlighting significant differences in profitability efficiency between these companies, with NVDA achieving a substantial margin despite its large revenue base. This disparity underscores the varying levels of operational efficiency and cost management among these industry leaders.
row3_heatmap: MA's 193.5% ROE stands out among peers, but its leverage risk is relatively low at 1.53x Debt/Equity. In contrast, JPM's high ROE of 17.1% is accompanied by significant leverage, highlighting a trade-off between returns and risk.
row4_market_trends: GOOGL's 12-month cumulative return of +107.9% stands out as the best performer among high-revenue companies. Conversely, ADBE's -42.1% return represents the worst performance in the same group. This significant divergence highlights contrasting fortunes within the sector.
